title: Well-posedness of the linear Cameron–Martin term ⟨ψ,u⟩_H under μ (μ ⟂ ν)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Step C1.** In the formal density $\frac{dT_\psi^*\mu}{d\mu}(u)=e^{\langle\psi,u\rangle_H^\sim-\frac12\|\psi\|_H^2}\,e^{V(u)-V_\psi(u)}$, with $H=H^1(\mathbb{T}^3)$, the linear stochastic term $\langle\psi,u\rangle_H^\sim$ is canonically an $L^2(\nu)$ object (Paley–Wiener extension for the GFF $\nu$). But the $\Phi^4_3$ measure $\mu$ is **mutually singular** with $\nu$ (Barashkov–Gubinelli). 

**Gap:** Prove $\langle\psi,u\rangle_H^\sim$ is well defined $\mu$-a.s. and in $L^2(\mu)$ for smooth $\psi$, and identify it concretely via the Wiener-chaos / scale decomposition used in the construction of $\mu$. Without this the Cameron–Martin shift term in the density is not even defined under $\mu$.

**Why nontrivial:** singularity w.r.t. $\nu$ forbids importing Gaussian Cameron–Martin verbatim. Smoothness of $\psi$ (so $\psi\in C^\infty\subset H^1$, in the GFF CM space) should make this the *easiest* renormalized object, but it must be established within the interacting-measure framework.
